current_state_summary.main_gap: The project is not yet Q1-SCI-ready because the strongest biological and drug-discovery claims still need large-scale real backend scoring, strong public baselines, matched-budget comparisons, statistical significance, raw-model attribution, and paper-level narrative/figures.
current_state_summary.risk_assessment: A Q1 medicinal chemistry, bioinformatics, or computational biology journal will likely reject the work if docking evidence remains proxy-only, if baselines are weak, if improvements are mostly repair/reranker-driven, or if experiments are too small for unseen-pocket generalization claims.
strengths: Rust-first modular framework with explicit topology, geometry, and pocket/context boundaries.
Layered generation artifacts now separate raw rollout, constrained/repaired outputs, and reranked candidates.
Candidate-level metric JSONL, correlation tables, artifact audit, claim contract, ablation summaries, and multi-seed summaries exist.
Vina and GNINA executables are installed locally, and RDKit-backed chemistry metrics are available.
Current evidence surface is more auditable than a typical prototype and avoids fabricated claim values.

global_rules: Do not fabricate or manually enter claim-bearing metrics.
Do not promote docking_like_score as Vina, GNINA, or binding affinity.
Do not claim raw generative-model improvement from repaired or reranked layers unless raw-layer evidence independently supports it.
Prefer Rust for core modeling and experiment infrastructure; Python tools are acceptable for backend adapters, artifact conversion, and paper table rendering.
Keep topology, geometry, and pocket/context encoders structurally separate.
Preserve gated cross-modality interaction; do not introduce naive unrestricted fusion.
Every task must leave a machine-readable artifact and a short human-readable summary when applicable.
After completing any task, remove that task from todo.json.
goal: Move the project from an internally auditable research prototype to a Q1-SCI-ready study with strong method novelty, rigorous unseen-pocket experiments, real backend evidence, matched baselines, statistically defensible claims, and publication-grade artifacts.

harder_targets_requiring_stronger_valida: Nature Machine Intelligence style venues require much broader novelty and external validation.
Medicinal chemistry Q1 journals may require stronger docking protocol validation, pose analysis, or wet-lab/MD support.
likely_fit_if_evidence_is_strong: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
journal_positioning_advice.recommended_angle: Position the paper as a modular, ablation-friendly, pocket-conditioned molecular generation framework with controlled cross-modal interaction and evidence-aware claim attribution, rather than only as another molecule generator.

minimum_publishable_package: Primary unseen-pocket benchmark with leakage checks.
Main method plus at least three credible baselines.
Three seeds for main method and primary baselines.
Candidate-level RDKit, Vina, GNINA, geometry, interaction, scaffold, and diversity metrics.
Artifact-generated paper tables and reproducibility manifest.

phases.goal: Create an honest, continuously updated gap report between current artifacts and Q1-SCI evidence requirements.
phases.name: Submission Readiness Audit

phases.goal: Replace proxy-only biological evidence with real, reproducible Vina/GNINA/RDKit candidate-level scoring.
phases.name: Real Backend Evidence

phases.goal: Define benchmark scope tightly enough for reproducible unseen-pocket claims.
phases.name: Benchmark And Splits

phases.goal: Show that the method is competitive against credible alternatives under identical evaluation conditions.
phases.name: Strong Baselines And Matched Budgets

phases.goal: Make improvements statistically defensible instead of anecdotal.
phases.name: Multi-Seed Statistics

phases.goal: Prove the research ideas matter: slot decomposition, redundancy reduction, semantic probes, leakage control, and gated cross-modal interaction.
phases.name: Ablations And Mechanistic Evidence

phases.goal: Explain what the model gets right and wrong, especially where proxy metrics disagree with docking or chemistry.
phases.name: Correlation, Failure, And Case Analysis

phases.goal: Improve the model only where evidence shows a real bottleneck.
phases.name: Method Improvement Only After Evidence

phases.goal: Turn artifacts into a coherent submission package.
phases.name: Manuscript And Reproducibility Package

stop_conditions: If real Vina/GNINA coverage remains below 70 percent after input preparation, do not submit Q1 claims about binding.
If strong baselines outperform the method on raw-flow and final-layer metrics, reposition the paper around auditability/modularity or improve the model before submission.
If gains disappear across seeds, downgrade the claim and focus on analysis or framework contribution.
If repair/reranking accounts for most improvement, state that explicitly and avoid claiming model-native generation superiority.
success_criteria_for_q1_submission_candi: At least one fixed unseen-pocket benchmark has complete generation, Vina/GNINA, RDKit, scaffold, interaction, and geometry artifacts.
At least three independent seeds are reported for the main method and all primary baselines.
At least three strong public baselines are compared under matched sampling budgets and identical evaluation backends.
Raw-flow, repaired, and reranked layer metrics are reported separately in every main result table.
Main claims include confidence intervals or hypothesis-test/effect-size statistics.
Correlation and failure analyses explain when geometry or pocket-fit proxies do or do not predict docking or drug-likeness.
Ablations isolate slot decomposition, redundancy reduction, semantic probes/leakage control, gated cross-attention, repair, and reranking.
The manuscript explicitly distinguishes model contribution from postprocessing contribution.
All reported tables and figures are generated from versioned artifacts by scripts.
